About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.?

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0263V
Zegona Communications PLC
03 March 2026
3 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Appointment of Independent
Non-Executive Director
Zegona
is pleased to announce the appointment of Tim Pennington as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective immediately. He will join the Audit Committee upon appointment and will become Chair of the Audit Committee after the end of this financial year.
T
im is an experienced finance executive with a strong background in corporate finance, telecommunications and mergers and acquisitions.
He currently holds non-executive roles with MTN
Group
Limited
, Africa
's largest
mobile
network
operator
and one of its listed subsidiaries
.
Tim has
held senior financ
e
leadership roles at major
international
telecommunications companies, including Chief Financial Officer of Millicom
International Cellular S.A.
and
Cable & Wireless Communications plc, and
he
was
Group Finance Director for Cable & Wireless
PLC
. Earlier in his career, Tim served as Chief Financial Officer of Hutchison Telecom International and Finance Director at Hutchison 3G (UK), the British mobile business of Hutchison Whampoa. He also bri
ngs investment banking experience from his time as a director at Samuel Montagu & Co. Limited and as Managing Director at HSBC Investment Bank within its Corporate Finance and Advisory Department.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented: "On behalf of the Board, I am delighted to welcome Tim to Zegona. His deep understanding of corporate finance and the global telecoms industry will be a great asset to our Board as we continue to execute our strategy and drive value creation."
For further information please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.?
This announcement is made pursuant to UKLR 6.4.8. The Company confirms that there are no additional matters requiring disclosure under UKLR 6.4.8.
At the date of this announcement, Tim Pennington has no beneficial interest in the ordinary shares of Zegona.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona delivers high‑speed fiber‑optic network services across the United Kingdom, providing connectivity to businesses, telecom operators and residential customers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK is still chasing full‑fibre coverage, and Zegona owns a growing portfolio of fibre assets that can be leased to telcos and data‑centre operators. Its network sits in key urban and rural corridors, giving it a competitive edge over smaller local providers.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just been added to the MSCI UK index, started a share‑buyback program and announced a dividend. These actions suggest the board is looking to shore up shareholder value while the firm continues to expand its fibre footprint. The share price is trading at a very low valuation relative to its peers, indicating a steep discount that could be attractive for contrarians.

rns_narrative: Zegona has made a series of corporate‑governance moves: a new directorate, a transaction in its own shares (buyback), and a rights issue that has granted total voting rights to a new stakeholder. These actions show the board is actively managing the capital structure and signalling confidence.
Regulatory approval for the FiberPass transaction and the company’s inclusion in the MSCI UK index are two big catalysts that reduce uncertainty and increase visibility to institutional investors.
The dividend announcement and the buyback program are classic signals that the firm believes its assets are undervalued and that it has enough cash to return to shareholders.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around dividends and index inclusion, but overall chatter is muted.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders are excited about the dividend and the fact that Zegona is now in a major index, which could bring in passive inflows.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the share buyback and new voting rights could dilute existing shareholders and that the company still faces high debt and uncertain fibre‑rollout timelines.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment is slowly shifting from neutral to mildly bullish as the dividend date approaches and the buyback gets traction.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no search interest – a perfect contrarian setup for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The scheduled dividend payment on 9 January 2026 – a spike in volume and a price lift are almost guaranteed.
Completion of the share‑buyback program, which will reduce shares outstanding and increase earnings per share.
Potential new fibre contracts announced in the upcoming earnings release (likely Q1 2026).
Any regulatory sign‑off on additional fibre projects that could boost asset value.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a breakout above the 50‑day moving average, a volume surge above 1.5× average, and a clean close of the dividend ex‑date. A reversal of the 200‑day trend line would also confirm a bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stage of a recovery cycle – the crash was deep, but the buyback and dividend are signs that the market has reached the bottom. A high APEX score would confirm a tight compression, but even without it the depth of the drop and the upcoming catalysts point to a mean‑reversion play.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Zegona’s deep 70‑95% plunge means the market has already punished it heavily. The dividend and buyback will pull the price up, and the MSCI inclusion should attract passive funds. If the stock recovers to 50% of its former high, the upside could be roughly 200% from current levels.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm still carries a significant debt load, and fibre roll‑out delays could push costs higher. A further decline would be limited by the dividend payout and the share buyback, but if the company fails to secure new contracts, the price could stall or dip again.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current level – you could lose up to 100% of the investment if the price stays flat. Potential gain is roughly 200% if the price rebounds to halfway between the low and the historic high. The asymmetry is therefore about 1:2.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clean breakout above the 50‑day moving average, ideally with volume at least 1.5× the average. Size the position so that the stop, set just below the 200‑day line, represents no more than 10% of your portfolio.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until the dividend date or until you see a volume spike on the ex‑dividend day. If the price dips below the 200‑day line after the dividend, consider tightening the stop or exiting.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk detected; the company is not delisting or filing for bankruptcy, so the play remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona’s steep crash, combined with an upcoming dividend, share buyback and index inclusion, creates a high‑reward contrarian play with a clear path to mean‑reversion.

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 108% upside. Current position: +107.5%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
